Councilman N. 8. Pratt's propo-
sition to take city parks out Of
politics and elect the commissions
at the same time school election
is held is being criticized on &C.
count Of fear that the combination
will allow political trading between
those interested in the two boards
concerned in the election.


But the 100000 clubs PROPOSE
ton is creating more adverse critic
ism even than Pratt's proposition
1t is. doubted if many of the several
thousand people read the petition
they signed to have the 100000
clubs proposition put to vote as
an amendment to the city charter.
This proposition would perpetuate
in complete charge of park affairs
10 men named by the club. 1t al
lows these men to appoint their
successors. Appointments are for
10 years.

ment the people would have no
